since before the pandemic for the man from trek segafredo the general classification looked like this with a seven second lead for richard canopas of ajay hindley with joao almeida down at 30 seconds in third place and five riders separated by only a minute and one second the closest the giro d'italia had been at this stage in 41 long years anticipation nerves at excitement were abound at the start of stage 16 in salo ahead of the 202 kilometer run all the way up to aprica one of the classic jira d'italia finishes and a tenth finish in the town another classic the mighty mortirolo climb this time from mono the slightly easier side and the valley cos santa cristina the final climb of the day that's coming just after a really tough intermediate sprint at taylor it was chaotic start mark cavadish matthew follow paul try to get up the road and out the way early on but they'd all be reeled in a group of 30 riders got up the road gilmar amongst them the most dangerous in the gc but the likes of balbara they were there alongside giulio chicorni who was fighting it out with kuhn balman for the mountains points he'd take the maximum points on the first climb of the day behind there were problems on descent for the likes of matija bais 106 kilometers to go riders who've been dropped getting back on and ineos grenadiers controlling things still having decent numbers at this stage but there were too many riders up the road to be in agreement eventually a small group managed to sneak off the front including leonard kemna already a stage winner on mount etna on to the more tirola problems for domenico puts of evil he was put under pressure and unfortunately hit the deck nebulae did take quite a bit of ground but eventually sat up when he saw that there was nobody going with him and it was a long way to the groups in front perhaps the mind games certainly a shot fired across the bows the pink jersey would be back on with pavel sivakov would have to have running repairs paul's was pulled back and it looked as though the pink jersey group was going to have something to say perhaps even for the stage win tim and irensman set off in such of glory and when he was still 40 seconds from the front and leonard kendra jan here also left valverde and carthy behind up the climb there'd be a bizarre crash people bow and mike lander touching wheels two teammates bilbao going down and then lander himself attacked further up karapas able to follow going with him would be jai hindley joe almeida looking like he was managing his effort the same for vincenzo nibili it was also off the pace kendo was caught and then he was passed by both ironsman and hit hit taking the soul lead with just under eight kilometers to go jan hit the czech rider back at the gyro d'italia and back on a stage including the moti rolo remember he'd finished second to giulio chicorni the last time the race climbed it and he was present were over a minute down the stage win was going to go to the breakaway joao almeida was limiting his losses as well just 15 seconds behind vincenzo nibali had probably lost over a minute by this point but was catching up a little on the descent into the final few hundred meters in africa jan hit winning just ahead of tim and irensman and finally after five visits to the judo nine grand tours jan hit a grand tour stage winner second place to young tim and limited his losses to inside a minute and the four bonus seconds changing the gc the difference now just three jan hit took all the glory a second stage win at this gyro for ando might have gone home
